title: Match window type to the aggregation intent
description: Choose tumbling, sliding, or session windows to fit whether periods are disjoint, overlapping, or activity-driven.
body: Use when defining how events group into windows. Do not default to a sliding window when you want disjoint periods — overlap double-counts events across windows. Actions: use tumbling windows for non-overlapping fixed intervals, sliding windows for moving averages where overlap is intended, and session windows keyed on inactivity gaps for user activity; verify an event lands in the expected window count. Validation signals include each event appearing in exactly the intended number of windows and aggregates matching a hand-computed reference. Risk boundary: a wrong window type silently inflates or splits aggregates; if results disagree with a manual check, treat the window choice as the defect before tuning anything else.
